auth_prerequisites: Provider credentials may be required for full automation runs after local install.

non_interactive_execution.summary: Public docs and PyPI distribution show a documented CLI install surface for OpenHands.
non_interactive_execution.notes: The package and docs provide a public execution entrypoint.

offline_strategy.summary: Wrapper tests can use fixtures or a fake OpenHands binary before any provider-backed automation run.
offline_strategy.notes: The recommendation lane only needs deterministic wrapper proof before later onboarding.

observable_cli_surface.summary: The docs and repo expose a public terminal-facing OpenHands surface.
observable_cli_surface.notes: Observable CLI behavior is grounded in public docs and upstream messaging.

redaction_fit.summary: Terminal output can traverse the repo redaction boundary before persistence.
redaction_fit.notes: Redaction fit is a repo-local inference from the external CLI posture.

crate_first_fit.summary: The installable external CLI maps directly onto a wrapper-crate-first onboarding stage.
crate_first_fit.notes: The repo can onboard the external CLI before any deeper backend work.

reproducibility.summary: Public docs and PyPI distribution make local setup reproducible before provider credentials are added.
reproducibility.notes: Local reproducibility is public even though full automation still needs credentials.

future_leverage.summary: OpenHands combines high adoption and active upstream movement, making it the strongest future-leverage finalist in this run.
future_leverage.notes: The upstream surface suggests the most strategic expansion potential among eligible finalists.

blocked_steps: Full provider-backed automation still needs maintainer credentials after local CLI setup.
normalized_caveats: Local install evidence is public now; provider-backed automation should be proven in onboarding.
